task_completion.description: Read /tmp/task_params.txt to identify the names of Patient A and Patient B.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor displaying the contents of /tmp/task_params.txt with the specific names of the two patients.

task_completion.description: Search for and open the medical chart for Patient A.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The EMR/EHR interface showing Patient A's chart open, typically with their name visible in the header or patient banner.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the relationships, family history, or contacts section of Patient A's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active tab or section in the EMR specifically dedicated to patient relationships or family members.

task_completion.description: Enter Patient B's actual name as the related person.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form or dialog for adding a relationship with Patient B's name (from the params file) typed into the target patient/person field.
A dropdown menu or text input field in the relationship form clearly showing 'Sibling' selected or entered.

task_completion.description: Save the new relationship and verify it is documented in the chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The saved relationship list showing Patient B linked to Patient A as a 'Sibling', ideally with a recent creation timestamp.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of the medical record system to add the relationship.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show clicking through the EMR interface, forms, and buttons, rather than executing database commands or API scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual patient names from the parameters file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The search and relationship forms contain real names (e.g., 'Ezekiel Walter'), not the literal placeholder strings 'Patient A' or 'Patient B'.

integrity.description: The relationship record was created genuinely and not spoofed.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are open to manipulate the DOM, and the UI looks consistent with the EMR application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The relationship type must be exactly 'Sibling'. The agent must use the actual names of Patient A and Patient B provided in /tmp/task_params.txt, not the literal strings 'Patient A' or 'Patient B'. The final relationship record should show a recent creation timestamp.
